questions.question: What is the key difference between values and goals?

options.text: Values and goals are the same thing
Values are ongoing directions that can never be 'completed,' while goals are specific achievements that can be checked off
Goals are more important than values
Values are only about career and work

questions.explanation: Values are ongoing directions, like heading North - you can always move toward them but never 'arrive' and stop. Goals are specific achievements (get a promotion, run a marathon) that can be completed. Values guide which goals to pursue and provide meaning beyond achievements.

questions.question: Why do values matter specifically for depression recovery?

options.text: They don't - values are only important when you're already feeling well
Depression disconnects us from values; reconnecting through values-aligned action creates meaning and counters the emptiness depression creates
Values help you make more money
Values are only useful for religious people

questions.explanation: Depression disconnects us from our values through anhedonia, fatigue, negative thinking, and avoidance. Acting in alignment with values - even without feeling the meaning - creates purpose, builds a life worth living, and counters the emptiness that depression creates.

questions.question: What are the seven life domains for exploring values?

options.text: Monday through Sunday
Relationships, Work/Career, Health, Learning/Growth, Leisure, Community, and Spirituality/Meaning
Eating, sleeping, working, exercising, socializing, relaxing, and commuting
Childhood, adolescence, young adulthood, adulthood, middle age, retirement, and end of life

questions.explanation: The seven life domains for values exploration are: Relationships (partner, friends, family), Work/Career, Health and Self-Care, Learning and Growth, Leisure and Recreation, Community and Service, and Spirituality and Meaning. Each domain offers opportunities to identify what matters most to you.

questions.question: What should you do if values-based activities feel empty or meaningless due to depression?

options.text: Stop doing them until you feel better
Do the activities anyway - feelings follow action, and meaning may return with time and practice
Only do activities that feel immediately rewarding
Conclude that your values have changed permanently

questions.explanation: Depression can make values feel hollow - this is a symptom, not reality. Continue doing values-based activities anyway because: (1) feelings follow action, not the other way around, (2) meaning often returns with time and repetition, (3) doing what aligns with who you want to be matters regardless of current feelings.

questions.question: When creating a balanced week, what are the minimum targets for different activity categories?

options.text: One activity total per day
Pleasure and Mastery daily, Social 2-3x/week, Physical 3-5x/week, Values-aligned daily
Only pleasure activities matter
Exercise 7 days a week, nothing else matters

questions.explanation: For a balanced week, aim for: at least one Pleasure activity daily, at least one Mastery activity daily, 2-3 Social activities per week, 3-5 Physical activities per week, and at least one Values-aligned activity daily. This ensures coverage across categories that support mood.

title: Reconnecting with Values and Activities - Quiz
